subject: Windows Python support
new: 32-bit Python on Windows is no longer supported
truth: Support for 32-bit Python on Windows has been dropped; users must install 64-bit Python.
quote: Note that support for 32-bit Python on Windows has been dropped in this release.

from_fact: 32-bit python on windows is no longer supported

subject: manifold.TSNE perplexity validation
old: TSNE accepted perplexity >= n_samples without error
new: TSNE now raises ValueError when perplexity >= n_samples
truth: TSNE now throws a ValueError when fit with perplexity >= n_samples.
quote: manifold.TSNE now throws a ValueError when fit with perplexity>=n_samples to ensure mathematical correctness of the algorithm.

from_fact: tsne accepted perplexity >= n_samples without error
tsne now raises valueerror when perplexity >= n_samples
associative: perplexity must be less than samples

subject: SelectFromModel default threshold for ElasticNet/LassoCV
old: SelectFromModel used default threshold (mean) for ElasticNet, ElasticNetCV with l1_ratio=1, and LassoCV
new: SelectFromModel defaults to selection threshold 1e-5 for those estimators
truth: SelectFromModel defaults to threshold 1e-5 when the estimator is ElasticNet, ElasticNetCV with l1_ratio=1, or LassoCV.
quote: threshold 1e-5 when the estimator is either :class:`linear_model.ElasticNet`

from_fact: selectfrommodel used default threshold (mean) for elasticnet, elasticnetcv with l1_ratio=1, and lassocv
selectfrommodel defaults to selection threshold 1e-5 for those estimators

subject: manifold.TSNE
new: throws ValueError when perplexity >= n_samples
truth: TSNE now raises a ValueError when perplexity is greater than or equal to n_samples.
quote: :class:`manifold.TSNE` now throws a `ValueError` when fit with `perplexity>=n_samples` to ensure mathematical correctness of the algorithm.

associative: perplexity must be less than samples

subject: metrics.precision_recall_curve
old: precision-recall curve did not include the final point at 100% recall
new: precision-recall curve now includes a last point at recall=100% with precision=class balance
truth: precision_recall_curve now appends a final point at 100% recall where precision equals the class balance, changing the curve shape and any downstream metrics derived from it.
quote: Fixes :func:`metrics.precision_recall_curve` to compute precision-recall at 100% recall. The Precision-Recall curve now displays the last point corresponding to a classifier that always predicts the positive class: recall=100% and precision=class balance.

from_fact: precision-recall curve did not include the final point at 100% recall
precision-recall curve now includes a last point at recall=100% with precision=class balance

subject: cluster.KMeans default algorithm
new: algorithm='lloyd'
truth: KMeans now defaults to algorithm='lloyd' instead of 'elkan'; Lloyd's algorithm uses less memory and is often faster but may produce different results due to numerical rounding.
quote: algorithm uses much less memory, and it is often faster.

subject: feature_selection.f_regression and r_regression default behaviour
old: returned np.nan and np.inf for some corner cases
new: returns finite score by default; use force_finite=False to get non-finite values
truth: f_regression and r_regression now return finite scores by default instead of np.nan/np.inf; pass force_finite=False to restore old behaviour.
quote: :func:`feature_selection.f_regression` and :func:`feature_selection.r_regression` will now return finite score by default instead of `np.nan` and `np.inf` for some corner case. You can use `force_finite=False` if you really want to get non-finite values and keep the old behavior.

from_fact: returned np.nan and np.inf for some corner cases
returns finite score by default; use force_finite=false to get non-finite values

subject: discriminant_analysis.LinearDiscriminantAnalysis variance scaling
new: uses correct variance-scaling coefficient
truth: LinearDiscriminantAnalysis now uses the correct variance-scaling coefficient, which may result in different model behaviour.
quote: :class:`discriminant_analysis.LinearDiscriminantAnalysis` now uses the correct variance-scaling coefficient which may result in different model behavior.

subject: preprocessing.KBinsDiscretizer bin edges
new: changed handling of bin edges slightly
truth: KBinsDiscretizer changed handling of bin edges, which might result in a different encoding with the same data.
quote: :class:`preprocessing.KBinsDiscretizer` changed handling of bin edges slightly, which might result in a different encoding with the same data.

from_fact: changed handling of bin edges slightly

subject: calibration.calibration_curve bin edges
new: changed handling of bin edges slightly
truth: calibration_curve changed handling of bin edges, which might result in a different output curve given the same data.
quote: :func:`calibration.calibration_curve` changed handling of bin edges slightly, which might result in a different output curve given the same data.

from_fact: changed handling of bin edges slightly

subject: tree.DecisionTreeClassifier, DecisionTreeRegressor, RandomForestClassifier, RandomForestRegressor, GradientBoostingClassifier, GradientBoostingRegressor split handling
old: old unstable sort algorithm for splits
new: new unstable sort algorithm for splits (15% faster)
truth: Tree-based models use a new sort algorithm that is 15% faster but may produce different models due to different handling of splits with tied criterion values.
quote: Fitting :class:`tree.DecisionTreeClassifier`, :class:`tree.DecisionTreeRegressor`, :class:`ensemble.RandomForestClassifier`, :class:`ensemble.RandomForestRegressor`, :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier`, and :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor` is on average 15% faster than in previous versions thanks to a new sort algorithm to find the best split. Models might be different because of a different handling of splits with tied criterion values: both the old and the new sorting algorithm are unstable sorting algorithms.

from_fact: old unstable sort algorithm for splits
new unstable sort algorithm for splits (15% faster)

subject: cluster.SpectralClustering and manifold.SpectralEmbedding eigenvector initialization
new: samples from a Gaussian when using 'amg' or 'lobpcg' solver
truth: SpectralClustering and SpectralEmbedding now sample eigenvectors from a Gaussian when using 'amg' or 'lobpcg' solver, improving numerical stability but may produce different models.
quote: The eigenvectors initialization for :class:`cluster.SpectralClustering` and :class:`manifold.SpectralEmbedding` now samples from a Gaussian when using the `'amg'` or `'lobpcg'` solver. This change improves numerical stability of the solver, but may result in a different model.

from_fact: samples from a gaussian when using 'amg' or 'lobpcg' solver

subject: ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier loss parameter
new: "log_loss"
truth: Stop using loss='deviance' for GradientBoostingClassifier; use loss='log_loss' instead.
quote: For :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier`, the `loss` parameter name "deviance" is deprecated in favor of the new name "log_loss", which is now the default.

subject: ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier loss parameter
new: "log_loss"
truth: Stop using loss='auto', 'binary_crossentropy', or 'categorical_crossentropy' for HistGradientBoostingClassifier; use loss='log_loss' instead.
quote: For :class:`ensemble.HistGradientBoostingClassifier`, the `loss` parameter names "auto", "binary_crossentropy" and "categorical_crossentropy" are deprecated in favor of the new name "log_loss", which is now the default.

subject: linear_model.SGDClassifier loss parameter
new: "log_loss"
truth: Stop using loss='log' for SGDClassifier; use loss='log_loss' instead.
quote: For :class:`linear_model.SGDClassifier`, the `loss` parameter name "log" is deprecated in favor of the new name "log_loss".

subject: Rich HTML representation in Jupyter
new: enabled by default
truth: Rich HTML representation of estimators is now enabled by default in Jupyter notebooks; set display='text' in sklearn.set_config to disable it.
quote: Rich html representation of estimators is now enabled by default in Jupyter notebooks. It can be deactivated by setting `display='text'` in :func:`sklearn.set_config`.

subject: calibration.calibration_curve normalize parameter
truth: Stop using the normalize parameter in calibration_curve; it is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3 — pass a proper probability for y_prob instead.
quote: :func:`calibration.calibration_curve`'s `normalize` parameter is now deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3. It is recommended that a proper probability (i.e. a classifier's :term:`predict_proba` positive class) is used for `y_prob`.

subject: cross_decomposition.CCA, PLSCanonical, PLSRegression coef_ shape
new: shape (n_targets, n_features) in version 1.3
truth: The coef_ attribute shape for CCA, PLSCanonical, and PLSRegression will change from (n_features, n_targets) to (n_targets, n_features) in version 1.3, breaking code that assumes the old shape.
quote: The shape of the :term:`coef_` attribute of :class:`cross_decomposition.CCA`, :class:`cross_decomposition.PLSCanonical` and :class:`cross_decomposition.PLSRegression` will change in version 1.3, from `(n_features, n_targets)` to `(n_targets, n_features)`, to be consistent with other linear models and to make it work with interface expecting a specific shape for `coef_` (e.g. :class:`feature_selection.RFE`).

subject: datasets.load_diabetes
old: load_diabetes() returned scaled data with exact previous results
new: load_diabetes(scaled=True) now computes scaled data from unscaled data, producing slightly different results (within 1e-4 absolute tolerance)
truth: datasets.load_diabetes() with scaled=True now computes scaled data from unscaled data, which may produce slightly different results than previous versions (within 1e-4 absolute tolerance).
quote: The scaled version of this dataset is now computed from the unscaled data, and can produce slightly different results than in previous version (within a 1e-4 absolute tolerance).

from_fact: load_diabetes() returned scaled data with exact previous results
load_diabetes(scaled=true) now computes scaled data from unscaled data, producing slightly different results (within 1e-4 absolute tolerance)

subject: decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning and decomposition.dict_learning_online
new: max_iter parameter
truth: Stop using n_iter; use max_iter instead for MiniBatchDictionaryLearning and dict_learning_online.
quote: the `n_iter` parameter of both is deprecated. Use `max_iter` instead.

subject: decomposition.dict_learning_online
truth: Stop using iter_offset, return_inner_stats, inner_stats, and return_n_iter parameters of dict_learning_online; they are deprecated.
quote: the `iter_offset`, `return_inner_stats`, `inner_stats` and `return_n_iter` parameters of :func:`decomposition.dict_learning_online` serve internal purpose and are deprecated.

subject: decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning
truth: Stop accessing inner_stats_, iter_offset_, and random_state_ attributes of MiniBatchDictionaryLearning; they are deprecated.
quote: the `inner_stats_`, `iter_offset_` and `random_state_` attributes of :class:`decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning` serve internal purpose and are deprecated.

subject: decomposition.MiniBatchDictionaryLearning and decomposition.dict_learning_online
new: default batch_size = 256 (in version 1.3)
truth: The default batch_size for MiniBatchDictionaryLearning and dict_learning_online will change from 3 to 256 in version 1.3, which will silently alter memory usage and convergence behaviour.
quote: the default value of the `batch_size` parameter of both will change from 3 to 256 in version 1.3.

subject: decomposition.KernelPCA
new: parameter validation in fit
truth: KernelPCA now validates input parameters in fit() instead of __init__; passing invalid parameters to the constructor will not raise an error until fit() is called.
quote: decomposition.KernelPCA now validates input parameters in fit instead of __init__.

subject: discriminant_analysis.LinearDiscriminantAnalysis
new: correct variance-scaling coefficient
truth: LinearDiscriminantAnalysis now uses a corrected variance-scaling coefficient, which may produce different model results compared to previous versions.
quote: discriminant_analysis.LinearDiscriminantAnalysis now uses the correct variance-scaling coefficient which may result in different model behavior.

subject: dummy.DummyRegressor
new: no longer overrides constant parameter during fit
truth: DummyRegressor no longer overrides the constant parameter during fit, so pre-set constant values are preserved.
quote: dummy.DummyRegressor no longer overrides the constant parameter during fit.

from_fact: no longer overrides constant parameter during fit

subject: decomposition.FastICA
new: whiten='unit-variance' (future default in 1.3)
truth: FastICA's whiten parameter default will change from True (arbitrary-variance) to 'unit-variance' in version 1.3; code relying on the old default behavior will break.
quote: The default value of its whiten argument will change from True (which behaves like 'arbitrary-variance') to 'unit-variance' in version 1.3.

subject: GradientBoostingClassifier/GradientBoostingRegressor validation_fraction
new: validation_fraction raises error if anything other than a float is passed
truth: Stop passing non-float values to validation_fraction in GradientBoostingClassifier and GradientBoostingRegressor; only float is accepted and anything else raises an error.
quote: Change the parameter `validation_fraction` in :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` and :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor` so that an error is raised if anything other than a float is passed in as an argument.

from_fact: validation_fraction raises error if anything other than a float is passed

subject: GradientBoostingClassifier/GradientBoostingRegressor loss_ attribute
truth: Stop accessing the loss_ attribute on GradientBoostingClassifier and GradientBoostingRegressor; it is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3.
quote: The attribute `loss_` of :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingClassifier` and :class:`ensemble.GradientBoostingRegressor` has been deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3.

subject: SequentialFeatureSelector n_features_to_select default
new: 'warn' (1.1), then 'auto' (1.3)
truth: Stop using n_features_to_select=None with SequentialFeatureSelector; it will be removed in 1.3. Use 'auto' or an integer instead.
quote: The default value of `n_features_to_select` changed from `None` to `'warn'` in 1.1 and will become `'auto'` in 1.3. `None` and `'warn'` will be removed in 1.3.

subject: SequentialFeatureSelector n_features_to_select None and 'warn'
truth: Stop passing n_features_to_select=None or 'warn' to SequentialFeatureSelector; both will be removed in 1.3. Use 'auto' or an integer.
quote: `None` and `'warn'` will be removed in 1.3.

subject: SimpleImputer verbose parameter
truth: Stop using the verbose parameter of SimpleImputer; it is deprecated and a warning will always be raised upon removal of empty columns.
quote: The `verbose` parameter was deprecated for :class:`impute.SimpleImputer`. A warning will always be raised upon the removal of empty columns.

subject: linear_model.RANSACRegressor parameter
new: estimator
truth: Stop using `base_estimator` parameter in `RANSACRegressor`; use `estimator` instead.
quote: Rename parameter `base_estimator` to `estimator` in :class:`linear_model.RANSACRegressor` to improve readability and consistency. `base_estimator` is deprecated and will be removed in 1.3.

subject: linear_model.LogisticRegression and LogisticRegressionCV n_iter_ attribute
old: n_iter_ recorded only the maximum number of iterations for each binary sub-problem in one-vs-rest
new: n_iter_ now records all iterations for each binary sub-problem
truth: LogisticRegression and LogisticRegressionCV n_iter_ now records all iterations per binary sub-problem instead of only the maximum.
quote: Previously, it would record only the maximum of the number of iterations for each binary sub-problem while now all of them are recorded.

from_fact: n_iter_ recorded only the maximum number of iterations for each binary sub-problem in one-vs-rest
n_iter_ now records all iterations for each binary sub-problem

subject: linear_model.GammaRegressor, PoissonRegressor, TweedieRegressor family property
old: family property was validated in __init__ and available
new: family property is deprecated and will be removed in 1.3
truth: The family property of GammaRegressor, PoissonRegressor, and TweedieRegressor is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3.
quote: produce the same models, but are deprecated and will be removed in version

from_fact: family property was validated in __init__ and available
family property is deprecated and will be removed in 1.3

subject: linear_model.LassoLarsIC AIC/BIC computation
old: AIC and BIC were computed incorrectly
new: AIC and BIC are now computed correctly; error raised when n_features > n_samples and noise variance not provided
truth: LassoLarsIC now correctly computes AIC and BIC, and raises an error when n_features > n_samples and noise variance is not provided.
quote: LassoLarsIC now correctly computes AIC and BIC. An error is now raised when n_features > n_samples and when the noise variance is not provided.

from_fact: aic and bic were computed incorrectly
aic and bic are now computed correctly; error raised when n_features > n_samples and noise variance not provided

subject: linear_model.TheilSenRegressor max_subpopulation validation
new: max_subpopulation is now validated in fit instead of __init__
truth: TheilSenRegressor now validates max_subpopulation in fit instead of __init__, so validation occurs later.
quote: TheilSenRegressor now validates input parameter max_subpopulation in fit instead of __init__.

from_fact: max_subpopulation is now validated in fit instead of __init__

subject: manifold.spectral_embedding
old: uniform on [0,1] random initial approximations to eigenvectors in eigen_solvers lobpcg and amg
new: Gaussian random initial approximations to eigenvectors in eigen_solvers lobpcg and amg
truth: Stop relying on uniform [0,1] random initialization for spectral_embedding with lobpcg/amg solvers; the initialization is now Gaussian, which changes the embedding results.
quote: manifold.spectral_embedding now uses Gaussian instead of the previous uniform on [0, 1] random initial approximations to eigenvectors in eigen_solvers lobpcg and amg to improve their numerical stability.

from_fact: uniform on [0,1] random initial approximations to eigenvectors in eigen_solvers lobpcg and amg
gaussian random initial approximations to eigenvectors in eigen_solvers lobpcg and amg

subject: metrics.SCORERS
new: metrics.get_scorer_names()
truth: Stop using metrics.SCORERS and use metrics.get_scorer_names() to retrieve available scorer names.
quote: use :func:`metrics.get_scorer_names` to retrieve the names of all available

subject: metrics.mean_absolute_percentage_error parameters
old: sample_weight and multioutput could be passed positionally
new: sample_weight and multioutput must be passed as keyword-only arguments
truth: Parameters sample_weight and multioutput of metrics.mean_absolute_percentage_error are now keyword-only and must be passed by name, not positionally.
quote: :func:`metrics.mean_absolute_percentage_error` are now keyword-only, in accordance

from_fact: sample_weight and multioutput could be passed positionally
sample_weight and multioutput must be passed as keyword-only arguments

subject: metrics.DistanceMetric 'wminkowski' metric
new: 'minkowski' metric with optional w parameter
truth: Stop using the 'wminkowski' metric of metrics.DistanceMetric; use 'minkowski' with an optional w parameter for weights instead.
quote: |API| The `"wminkowski"` metric of :class:`metrics.DistanceMetric` is deprecated

subject: metrics.DistanceMetric import location
new: sklearn.metrics.DistanceMetric
truth: Stop importing DistanceMetric from sklearn.neighbors; import from sklearn.metrics instead. The neighbors alias will be removed in 1.3.
quote: :mod:`sklearn.neighbors` to :mod:`sklearn.metrics`.

subject: model_selection.GridSearchCV
new: validates input parameters in fit instead of __init__
truth: GridSearchCV and HalvingGridSearchCV now validate input parameters in fit() instead of __init__(); code that relied on validation errors being raised at construction time will break.
quote: :class:`model_selection.GridSearchCV`, :class:`model_selection.HalvingGridSearchCV` now validates input parameters in `fit` instead of `__init__`.

from_fact: validates input parameters in fit instead of __init__

subject: pipeline.Pipeline
new: validates hyper-parameters in .fit() instead of __init__
truth: Pipeline no longer validates hyper-parameters in __init__; validation happens in .fit() instead, so invalid params won't raise errors until fit is called.
quote: :class:`pipeline.Pipeline` now does not validate hyper-parameters in `__init__` but in `.fit()`.

subject: pipeline.FeatureUnion
new: validates hyper-parameters in .fit() and .fit_transform() instead of __init__
truth: FeatureUnion no longer validates hyper-parameters in __init__; validation is now handled in .fit() and .fit_transform(), so invalid params won't raise errors at construction time.
quote: :class:`pipeline.FeatureUnion` does not validate hyper-parameters in `__init__`. Validation is now handled in `.fit()` and `.fit_transform()`.

from_fact: validates hyper-parameters in .fit() and .fit_transform() instead of __init__

subject: neighbors.KernelDensity
new: validates input parameters in fit instead of __init__
truth: KernelDensity now validates input parameters in fit() instead of __init__(); code that relied on validation errors at construction time will break.
quote: :class:`neighbors.KernelDensity` now validates input parameters in `fit` instead of `__init__`.

from_fact: validates input parameters in fit instead of __init__

subject: svm.SVR, svm.SVC, svm.NuSVR, svm.OneClassSVM, svm.NuSVC
old: previously allowed non-finite parameter weights from dual-gap estimation
new: now raise an error when dual-gap estimation produces non-finite parameter weights
truth: svm.SVR, svm.SVC, svm.NuSVR, svm.OneClassSVM, and svm.NuSVC now raise an error when dual-gap estimation produces non-finite parameter weights, instead of silently proceeding.
quote: when the dual-gap estimation produces non-finite parameter weights.

from_fact: previously allowed non-finite parameter weights from dual-gap estimation
now raise an error when dual-gap estimation produces non-finite parameter weights

subject: svm.NuSVC, svm.NuSVR, svm.SVC, svm.SVR, svm.OneClassSVM
new: input parameters validated in fit instead of __init__
truth: svm.NuSVC, svm.NuSVR, svm.SVC, svm.SVR, and svm.OneClassSVM now validate input parameters in fit() instead of __init__(), so parameter errors surface later.
quote: now validates input parameters in `fit` instead of `__init__`.

from_fact: input parameters validated in fit instead of __init__

new: default max_features is 1.0
truth: The default value of max_features for ExtraTreeRegressor changed from 'auto' to 1.0.

new: default max_features is 'sqrt'

truth: Setting max_features='auto' is deprecated for DecisionTreeClassifier, DecisionTreeRegressor, ExtraTreeClassifier, and ExtraTreeRegressor, and will be removed in version 1.3.

subject: if_delegate_has_method

old: In version 1.2, min_sample_split=1 did not raise an error

truth: Stop passing min_sample_split=1 to tree classifiers/regressors; it now raises an error.
quote: :class:`tree.ExtraTreeRegressor` where an error was no longer raised in version

new: maintains the boolean dtype of pandas Series

quote: Restore :func:`utils.check_array`'s behaviour for pandas Series of type boolean. The type is maintained, instead of converting to `float64.`

subject: utils.fixes.delayed

old: old condition did not disambiguate training vs validation set and overscaled error tolerance
new: fixed condition properly distinguishes training and validation sets

new: n_init='auto' in 1.4

subject: SpectralClustering and spectral_clustering eigen_tol default

subject: decomposition.MiniBatchSparsePCA n_iter parameter
new: max_iter, tol, max_no_improvement
truth: Stop using n_iter in MiniBatchSparsePCA; use max_iter, tol, and max_no_improvement instead. n_iter is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.3.
quote: controlled by the new `max_iter`, `tol` and `max_no_improvement` parameters.

subject: linear_model.QuantileRegressor solver default
new: solver='highs'
truth: Stop using solver='interior-point' as default; the default solver for QuantileRegressor will change to 'highs' in version 1.4.
quote: The default value for the `solver` parameter in :class:`linear_model.QuantileRegressor` will change from `"interior-point"` to `"highs"` in version 1.4.

subject: linear_model.LogisticRegression penalty argument
new: penalty=None
truth: Stop using string 'none' for penalty in LogisticRegression; use Python None instead.
quote: String option `"none"` is deprecated for `penalty` argument in :class:`linear_model.LogisticRegression`, and will be removed in version 1.4. Use `None` instead.

subject: manifold.SpectralEmbedding eigen_tol default
new: eigen_tol='auto'
truth: The default eigen_tol for SpectralEmbedding and spectral_embedding is changing from 0 to 'auto' in version 1.3; the old default 0 is deprecated.
quote: Includes a new option `eigen_tol="auto"` and begins deprecation to change the default from `eigen_tol=0` to `eigen_tol="auto"` in version 1.3.

subject: metrics.log_loss
new: eps default is now 'auto'
truth: Stop relying on eps defaulting to 1e-15; it now defaults to 'auto' which sets eps based on y_pred dtype.
quote: the default value of `eps` is changed from `1e-15` to the new `"auto"` option.

subject: metrics.ndcg_score
old: negative y_true values were silently accepted with possibly out-of-range results
new: negative y_true values now trigger a warning, and will raise an error in v1.4
truth: Stop passing negative values to y_true in ndcg_score; they now warn and will error in v1.4.
quote: `metrics.ndcg_score` will now trigger a warning when the `y_true` value contains a negative value. Users may still use negative values, but the result may not be between 0 and 1. Starting in v1.4, passing in negative values for `y_true` will raise an error.

from_fact: negative y_true values were silently accepted with possibly out-of-range results
negative y_true values now trigger a warning, and will raise an error in v1.4

subject: metrics.pairwise.manhattan_distances
new: removed in v1.4 (deprecated now)
truth: Stop using sum_over_features in manhattan_distances; it is deprecated and will be removed in v1.4.
quote: The parameter `base_estimator` is deprecated and will be removed in 1.4.

subject: manifold.MDS / manifold.smacof
new: normalized_stress now has 'auto' option which will become default in v1.4
truth: Stop relying on normalized_stress defaulting to a fixed value; 'auto' will become the default in v1.4, enabling it only when metric=False.
quote: `"auto"` will become the default value for `normalized_stress` in version 1.4.

from_fact: normalized_stress now has 'auto' option which will become default in v1.4

subject: SearchCV rank for NaN scores
old: rank for NaN scores was set to np.iinfo(np.int32).min (with scipy >= 1.10)
new: rank for NaN scores is set to the maximum possible rank
truth: For all SearchCV classes with scipy >= 1.10, NaN scores now get the maximum possible rank instead of np.iinfo(np.int32).min.
quote: For all `SearchCV` classes and scipy >= 1.10, rank corresponding to a nan score is correctly set to the maximum possible rank, rather than `np.iinfo(np.int32).min`.

from_fact: rank for nan scores was set to np.iinfo(np.int32).min (with scipy >= 1.10)
rank for nan scores is set to the maximum possible rank

subject: HalvingGridSearchCV and HalvingRandomSearchCV NaN score ranking
old: parameter combinations with NaN scores did not share the lowest rank
new: parameter combinations with NaN scores now share the lowest rank
truth: In HalvingGridSearchCV and HalvingRandomSearchCV, parameter combinations with a NaN score now share the lowest rank.
quote: In both :class:`model_selection.HalvingGridSearchCV` and :class:`model_selection.HalvingRandomSearchCV` parameter combinations with a NaN score now share the lowest rank.

from_fact: parameter combinations with nan scores did not share the lowest rank
parameter combinations with nan scores now share the lowest rank

subject: GridSearchCV and RandomizedSearchCV NaN score ranking
old: ranks for NaN scores were not all set to the maximum possible rank
new: ranks for NaN scores are all set to the maximum possible rank
truth: For GridSearchCV and RandomizedSearchCV, ranks corresponding to NaN scores will all be set to the maximum possible rank.
quote: For :class:`model_selection.GridSearchCV` and :class:`model_selection.RandomizedSearchCV` ranks corresponding to nan scores will all be set to the maximum possible rank.

from_fact: ranks for nan scores were not all set to the maximum possible rank
ranks for nan scores are all set to the maximum possible rank

subject: KNeighborsTransformer and RadiusNeighborsTransformer default n_jobs
new: n_jobs=None by default
truth: KNeighborsTransformer and RadiusNeighborsTransformer now default to n_jobs=None instead of n_jobs=1.
quote: Set `n_jobs=None` by default (instead of `1`) for :class:`neighbors.KNeighborsTransformer` and :class:`neighbors.RadiusNeighborsTransformer`.

subject: MLPClassifier and MLPRegressor parameters
old: best_loss_, validation_scores_, best_validation_score_ were not always exposed or had different None behavior
new: best_loss_ is None when early_stopping=True; validation_scores_ and best_validation_score_ are None when early_stopping=False
truth: Stop relying on best_loss_ having a value when early_stopping=True, and stop relying on validation_scores_ or best_validation_score_ having values when early_stopping=False — they are now None in those cases.
quote: best_loss_ is set to None when early_stopping=True, while validation_scores_ and best_validation_score_ are set to None when early_stopping=False.

from_fact: best_loss_, validation_scores_, best_validation_score_ were not always exposed or had different none behavior
best_loss_ is none when early_stopping=true; validation_scores_ and best_validation_score_ are none when early_stopping=false

subject: OneHotEncoder sparse parameter
new: sparse_output
truth: Stop using the sparse parameter of OneHotEncoder; use sparse_output instead.
quote: |API| The `sparse` parameter of :class:`preprocessing.OneHotEncoder`

subject: class_weight_ attribute on NuSVR, SVR, OneClassSVM
new: class_weight_ attribute is deprecated
truth: Stop accessing the class_weight_ attribute on NuSVR, SVR, and OneClassSVM — it is deprecated.
quote: |API| The `class_weight_` attribute is now deprecated for

subject: utils.extmath.density
truth: Stop passing extra keyword parameters to utils.extmath.density; they are deprecated and will be removed in version 1.4.
quote: |API| The extra keyword parameters of :func:`utils.extmath.density` are deprecated

subject: Ridge with solver='sparse_cg'
old: Ridge models with solver='sparse_cg' produced consistent results across scipy versions
new: Ridge models with solver='sparse_cg' may produce slightly different results with scipy>=1.12
truth: Ridge models using solver='sparse_cg' may produce different results when run with scipy>=1.12 due to underlying changes in the scipy solver.
quote: Ridge models with `solver='sparse_cg'` may have slightly different results with scipy>=1.12, because of an underlying change in the scipy solver

from_fact: ridge models with solver='sparse_cg' produced consistent results across scipy versions
ridge models with solver='sparse_cg' may produce slightly different results with scipy>=1.12

subject: PLSRegression predict output shape
old: PLSRegression.predict() returned a 2D array when fitted with 1D y
new: PLSRegression.predict() now automatically ravels the output to 1D when fitted with 1D y
truth: PLSRegression.predict() now returns a 1D array instead of a 2D array when the model was fitted with a one-dimensional y.
quote: PLSRegression now automatically ravels the output of `predict` if fitted with one dimensional `y`.

from_fact: plsregression.predict() returned a 2d array when fitted with 1d y
plsregression.predict() now automatically ravels the output to 1d when fitted with 1d y

subject: ensemble.AdaBoostClassifier with algorithm='SAMME'
old: decision function of each weak learner was not symmetric (scores did not sum to zero for a sample)
new: decision function of each weak learner is now symmetric (scores sum to zero for a sample)
truth: AdaBoostClassifier with algorithm='SAMME' now enforces symmetric decision functions where weak learner scores sum to zero per sample, changing model outputs.
quote: where the decision function of each weak learner should be symmetric (i.e.

from_fact: decision function of each weak learner was not symmetric (scores did not sum to zero for a sample)
decision function of each weak learner is now symmetric (scores sum to zero for a sample)

subject: mixture.GaussianMixture with user-provided precisions_init
old: initialization from user-provided precisions_init for covariance_type 'full' or 'tied' was incorrect
new: initialization from user-provided precisions_init for covariance_type 'full' or 'tied' is now correct
truth: GaussianMixture with user-provided precisions_init for covariance_type='full' or 'tied' now initializes correctly, changing resulting model.
quote: `precisions_init` for `covariance_type` of `full` or `tied` was not correct,

from_fact: initialization from user-provided precisions_init for covariance_type 'full' or 'tied' was incorrect
initialization from user-provided precisions_init for covariance_type 'full' or 'tied' is now correct

subject: feature_selection.mutual_info_regression with integer dtype X
old: did not correctly compute result when X is of integer dtype
new: now correctly computes result when X is of integer dtype
truth: mutual_info_regression now produces different (correct) results when X has integer dtype.
quote: feature_selection.mutual_info_regression now correctly computes the result when X is of integer dtype.

from_fact: did not correctly compute result when x is of integer dtype
now correctly computes result when x is of integer dtype

subject: impute.KNNImputer with add_indicator=True
old: missing indicator column was not correctly added in transform when add_indicator=True and missing values observed during fit
new: missing indicator column is now correctly added in transform
truth: KNNImputer with add_indicator=True now correctly adds the missing indicator column in transform, changing output shape/content.
quote: KNNImputer now correctly adds a missing indicator column in transform when add_indicator is set to True and missing values are observed during fit.

from_fact: missing indicator column was not correctly added in transform when add_indicator=true and missing values observed during fit
missing indicator column is now correctly added in transform

subject: neighbors.KNeighborsClassifier.predict with pandas DataFrame input
old: raised an exception for pandas.DataFrame input
new: no longer raises an exception for pandas.DataFrame input
truth: KNeighborsClassifier.predict now accepts pandas DataFrame input without raising an exception.
quote: neighbors.KNeighborsClassifier.predict no longer raises an exception for pandas.DataFrames input.

from_fact: raised an exception for pandas.dataframe input
no longer raises an exception for pandas.dataframe input

subject: neighbors based estimators with metric='minkowski' and 0 < p < 1
old: did not work correctly depending on dtype of X
new: now correctly work regardless of dtype of X
truth: Neighbors-based estimators with metric='minkowski' and p in (0,1) now work correctly for all dtypes of X, changing previous behavior.
quote: |Fix| Neighbors based estimators now correctly work when `metric="minkowski"` and the

from_fact: did not work correctly depending on dtype of x
now correctly work regardless of dtype of x

subject: preprocessing.LabelEncoder accepting y as keyword argument
old: did not correctly accept y as a keyword argument
new: now correctly accepts y as a keyword argument
truth: LabelEncoder now correctly accepts y as a keyword argument, changing how it can be called.
quote: preprocessing.LabelEncoder correctly accepts y as a keyword argument.

from_fact: did not correctly accept y as a keyword argument
now correctly accepts y as a keyword argument

subject: tree.plot_tree accepting class_names=True
old: did not accept class_names=True as documented
new: now accepts class_names=True as documented
truth: plot_tree now accepts class_names=True, changing previous behavior where it was rejected.
quote: tree.plot_tree now accepts class_names=True as documented.

from_fact: did not accept class_names=true as documented

subject: tree.plot_tree feature_names parameter
new: now accepts any kind of array-like
truth: plot_tree's feature_names parameter now accepts any array-like instead of only a list.
quote: |Fix| The `feature_names` parameter of :func:`tree.plot_tree` now accepts any kind of

from_fact: now accepts any kind of array-like

subject: model_selection.HalvingRandomSearchCV with param_distributions as list of dicts
old: raised an error when param_distributions was a list of dicts
new: no longer raises an error
truth: HalvingRandomSearchCV now accepts param_distributions as a list of dicts without raising an error.
quote: HalvingRandomSearchCV no longer raises when the input to the param_distributions parameter is a list of dicts.

from_fact: raised an error when param_distributions was a list of dicts

subject: multiclass.OutputCodeClassifier.predict
new: different tie-breaking strategy due to more efficient pairwise distance reduction
truth: OutputCodeClassifier.predict may return different labels because the tie-breaking strategy changed.
quote: the tie-breaking strategy is different and thus the predicted labels may be different.

from_fact: different tie-breaking strategy due to more efficient pairwise distance reduction

subject: decomposition.DictionaryLearning.fit_transform
old: previous results when transform_algorithm differs from fit_algorithm with few iterations
new: more efficient but may produce different results under those conditions
truth: DictionaryLearning.fit_transform may produce different results when transform_algorithm differs from fit_algorithm and iteration count is small.
quote: may produce different results as in previous versions when transform_algorithm is not the same as fit_algorithm and the number of iterations is small.

from_fact: previous results when transform_algorithm differs from fit_algorithm with few iterations
more efficient but may produce different results under those conditions

subject: cluster.KMeans, cluster.BisectingKMeans, cluster.MiniBatchKMeans
old: sample_weight not used in centroid initialization
new: sample_weight now used in centroid initialization
truth: KMeans, BisectingKMeans, and MiniBatchKMeans now use sample_weight in centroid initialization, so same random seeds produce different numbers.
quote: initialization for :class:`cluster.KMeans`, :class:`cluster.BisectingKMeans`

from_fact: sample_weight not used in centroid initialization
sample_weight now used in centroid initialization

subject: decomposition.NMF and decomposition.MiniBatchNMF
old: treatment of small values in W and H matrices
new: more consistent treatment of small values in W and H matrices
truth: NMF and MiniBatchNMF now treat small values in W and H matrices more consistently, which can produce different results than previous versions.
quote: :class:`decomposition.MiniBatchNMF` which can produce different results than previous

from_fact: treatment of small values in w and h matrices
more consistent treatment of small values in w and h matrices

subject: decomposition.KernelPCA.inverse_transform
old: when gamma is None, gamma was incorrectly chosen as 1/n_features of data passed to inverse_transform
new: gamma is correctly chosen as 1/n_features of the data it is fitted on
truth: KernelPCA.inverse_transform with gamma=None now uses gamma based on the fitted data, not the data passed to inverse_transform, so results may differ.
quote: may produce different results through inverse_transform if gamma is None. Now it will be chosen correctly as 1/n_features of the data that it is fitted on, while previously it might be incorrectly chosen as 1/n_features of the data passed to inverse_transform.

from_fact: when gamma is none, gamma was incorrectly chosen as 1/n_features of data passed to inverse_transform
gamma is correctly chosen as 1/n_features of the data it is fitted on

subject: model_selection.LearningCurveDisplay
old: default score_type did not show both train and test curves
new: displays both train and test curves by default
truth: LearningCurveDisplay now shows both train and test curves by default; set score_type='test' to restore old behaviour.
quote: train and test curves by default.

from_fact: default score_type did not show both train and test curves
displays both train and test curves by default

subject: get_feature_names_out on multiple estimators
old: did not raise error when instance not fitted
new: raises NotFittedError if instance is not fitted
truth: get_feature_names_out now raises NotFittedError if the estimator is not fitted, instead of silently returning or behaving differently.
quote: raises a `NotFittedError` if the instance is not fitted.

from_fact: did not raise error when instance not fitted
raises notfittederror if instance is not fitted

subject: OpenMP thread count default
new: number of physical cores
truth: All estimators relying on OpenMP multi-threading now use the number of physical cores instead of logical cores as the default thread count.
quote: All estimators that internally rely on OpenMP multi-threading (via Cython) now use a number of threads equal to the number of physical (instead of logical) cores by default.

subject: sample_weight in KMeans/BisectingKMeans/MiniBatchKMeans centroid initialization
old: sample_weight was not used in centroid initialization
new: sample_weight is now used in centroid initialization
truth: sample_weight now affects centroid initialization in KMeans, BisectingKMeans, and MiniBatchKMeans, so results from the same random seed will differ.
quote: The `sample_weight` parameter now will be used in centroids initialization for :class:`cluster.KMeans`, :class:`cluster.BisectingKMeans` and :class:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans`. This change will break backward compatibility, since numbers generated from same random seeds will be different.

from_fact: sample_weight was not used in centroid initialization
sample_weight is now used in centroid initialization

subject: sample_weight parameter in KMeans.predict and MiniBatchKMeans.predict
new: deprecated, will be removed in v1.5
truth: Stop using sample_weight in KMeans.predict() and MiniBatchKMeans.predict(); it is deprecated and will be removed in v1.5.
quote: The `sample_weight` parameter in `predict` for :meth:`cluster.KMeans.predict` and :meth:`cluster.MiniBatchKMeans.predict` is now deprecated and will be removed in v1.5.

from_fact: deprecated, will be removed in v1.5

subject: Xred argument in FeatureAgglomeration.inverse_transform
new: Xt parameter
truth: Stop using Xred in FeatureAgglomeration.inverse_transform(); use Xt instead. Xred will be removed in v1.5.
quote: The `Xred` argument in :func:`cluster.FeatureAgglomeration.inverse_transform` is renamed to `Xt` and will be removed in v1.5.

subject: cov_init parameter in covariance.graphical_lasso
new: deprecated, has no effect, will be removed in 1.5
truth: Stop using cov_init in covariance.graphical_lasso(); it has no effect and is deprecated, to be removed in 1.5.
quote: Deprecates `cov_init` in :func:`covariance.graphical_lasso` in 1.3 since the parameter has no effect. It will be removed in 1.5.

from_fact: deprecated, has no effect, will be removed in 1.5

subject: ARFF missing value marker in fetch_openml with pandas parser
old: Previously, multiple markers or different markers may have been treated as missing values when opening ARFF files with the pandas parser.
new: Only the marker "?" is now considered as a missing value when opening ARFF files fetched using datasets.fetch_openml with the pandas parser.
truth: When using the pandas parser with datasets.fetch_openml, only the marker "?" is treated as a missing value; other markers are no longer automatically treated as missing.
quote: Following the ARFF specs, only the marker "?" is now considered as a missing values when opening ARFF files fetched using :func:`datasets.fetch_openml` when using the pandas parser.

from_fact: previously, multiple markers or different markers may have been treated as missing values when opening arff files with the pandas parser.
only the marker "?" is now considered as a missing value when opening arff files fetched using datasets.fetch_openml with the pandas parser.

subject: Missing marker consistency in fetch_openml
old: The missing marker may have differed between the "pandas" and "liac-arff" parsers.
new: Both parsers ("pandas" and "liac-arff") now consistently use np.nan as the missing marker.
truth: datasets.fetch_openml now consistently uses np.nan as the missing marker with both the "pandas" and "liac-arff" parsers.
quote: :func:`datasets.fetch_openml` will consistently use `np.nan` as missing marker with both parsers "pandas" and "liac-arff".

from_fact: the missing marker may have differed between the "pandas" and "liac-arff" parsers.
both parsers ("pandas" and "liac-arff") now consistently use np.nan as the missing marker.

subject: data_transposed argument of datasets.make_sparse_coded_signal
truth: The data_transposed argument of datasets.make_sparse_coded_signal is deprecated and will be removed in v1.5; stop using it.
quote: The `data_transposed` argument of :func:`datasets.make_sparse_coded_signal` is deprecated and will be removed in v1.5.

subject: NMF and MiniBatchNMF fit/transform results for small W/H values
old: Small values in W and H matrices were treated differently during fit and transform.
new: Small values in W and H matrices are now treated more consistently during fit and transform, which can produce different results than previous versions.
truth: Small values in the W and H matrices during fit and transform of decomposition.NMF and decomposition.MiniBatchNMF are now treated more consistently, which can produce different results than previous versions.
quote: Treat more consistently small values in the `W` and `H` matrices during the `fit` and `transform` steps of :class:`decomposition.NMF` and :class:`decomposition.MiniBatchNMF` which can produce different results than previous versions.

from_fact: small values in w and h matrices were treated differently during fit and transform.
small values in w and h matrices are now treated more consistently during fit and transform, which can produce different results than previous versions.

subject: W argument in NMF.inverse_transform and MiniBatchNMF.inverse_transform
new: The argument is renamed to Xt.
truth: The W argument in decomposition.NMF.inverse_transform and decomposition.MiniBatchNMF.inverse_transform is renamed to Xt; use Xt instead of W.
quote: The `W` argument in :func:`decomposition.NMF.inverse_transform` and :class:`decomposition.MiniBatchNMF.inverse_transform` is renamed to `Xt` and will be removed in v1.5.

from_fact: the argument is renamed to xt.

subject: IsolationForest predict with pre-1.3 models
old: An estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 could be loaded and used for predict with scikit-learn 1.3.
new: It is not possible to load an estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 to make it predict with scikit-learn 1.3; retraining with 1.3 is required.
truth: It is not possible to load an IsolationForest estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 to make predictions with scikit-learn 1.3; you must retrain with 1.3.
quote: It is therefore not possible to load an estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 to make it predict with scikit-learn 1.3: retraining with scikit-learn 1.3 is required.

from_fact: an estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 could be loaded and used for predict with scikit-learn 1.3.
it is not possible to load an estimator trained with scikit-learn 1.2 to make it predict with scikit-learn 1.3; retraining with 1.3 is required.

subject: RandomForestClassifier and RandomForestRegressor with warm_start=True
old: out-of-bag scores recomputed on every fit call
new: out-of-bag scores only recomputed when there are more n_estimators in subsequent fit calls
truth: RandomForestClassifier and RandomForestRegressor with warm_start=True only recompute out-of-bag scores when subsequent fit calls actually increase n_estimators.
quote: recomputes out-of-bag scores when there are actually more `n_estimators`

from_fact: out-of-bag scores recomputed on every fit call
out-of-bag scores only recomputed when there are more n_estimators in subsequent fit calls

subject: HistGradientBoostingRegressor and HistGradientBoostingClassifier
old: negative values for categorical features were treated as a separate category or caused errors
new: negative values for categorical features are treated consistently as missing values
truth: HistGradientBoostingRegressor and HistGradientBoostingClassifier now treat negative values for categorical features as missing values, following LightGBM and pandas conventions.
quote: categorical features consistently as missing values, following LightGBM's and

from_fact: negative values for categorical features were treated as a separate category or caused errors
negative values for categorical features are treated consistently as missing values

subject: PatchExtractor
old: did not follow the transformer API (no fit/transform contract)
new: now follows the transformer API as a stateless transformer; parameter validation only happens at fit time
truth: PatchExtractor now follows the transformer API: it is a stateless transformer requiring fit before transform, and parameter validation only occurs at fit time.
quote: feature_extraction.image.PatchExtractor now follows the transformer API of scikit-learn. This class is defined as a stateless transformer meaning that it is not required to call fit before calling transform. Parameter validation only happens at fit time.

from_fact: did not follow the transformer api (no fit/transform contract)
now follows the transformer api as a stateless transformer; parameter validation only happens at fit time

subject: kernel_approximation.AdditiveChi2Sampler
new: removed (stateless)
truth: Stop using the `sample_interval_` attribute on `AdditiveChi2Sampler`; it is deprecated and will be removed in 1.5 as the estimator is now stateless.
quote: The `sample_interval_` attribute is deprecated and will be removed in 1.5.

subject: linear_model.BayesianRidge and linear_model.ARDRegression
new: max_iter parameter
truth: Stop using `n_iter` in `BayesianRidge` and `ARDRegression`; use `max_iter` instead.
quote: the `n_iter` parameter of both is deprecated.

subject: metrics.classification_report
old: macro avg and weighted avg returned inconsistent values (f1-score=np.nan vs f1-score=0.0) for empty input
new: both return np.nan
truth: metrics.classification_report now returns np.nan for both macro avg and weighted avg on empty input instead of 0.0 for weighted avg
quote: Fixed :func:`metrics.classification_report` so that empty input will return `np.nan`. Previously, "macro avg" and `weighted avg` would return e.g. `f1-score=np.nan` and `f1-score=0.0`, being inconsistent. Now, they both return `np.nan`.

from_fact: macro avg and weighted avg returned inconsistent values (f1-score=np.nan vs f1-score=0.0) for empty input

subject: metrics.log_loss
new: raises a warning if y_pred values are not normalized; starting from 1.5 will raise an error
truth: metrics.log_loss no longer normalizes y_pred automatically and will warn; in version 1.5 it will raise an error instead
quote: :func:`metrics.log_loss` raises a warning if the values of the parameter `y_pred` are not normalized, instead of actually normalizing them in the metric. Starting from 1.5 this will raise an error.

from_fact: raises a warning if y_pred values are not normalized; starting from 1.5 will raise an error
associative: log loss y_pred sum not one

subject: metrics.log_loss eps parameter
truth: The eps parameter of metrics.log_loss has been deprecated and will be removed in version 1.5
quote: The `eps` parameter of the :func:`metrics.log_loss` has been deprecated and will be removed in 1.5.

subject: metrics 'matching' metric
old: 'matching' metric was available when using SciPy>=1.9
truth: The 'matching' metric has been removed when using SciPy>=1.9
quote: The `'matching'` metric has been removed when using SciPy>=1.9 to be consistent with `scipy.spatial.distance` which does not support `'matching'` anymore.

from_fact: 'matching' metric was available when using scipy>=1.9

subject: model_selection.LearningCurveDisplay.plot
truth: Stop using the log_scale parameter in LearningCurveDisplay.plot; set the scale directly on the ax object instead.
quote: The parameter `log_scale` in the method `plot` of the class :class:`model_selection.LearningCurveDisplay` has been deprecated in 1.3 and will be removed in 1.5. The default scale can be overridden by setting it directly on the `ax` object and will be set automatically from the spacing of the data points otherwise.

subject: neighbors.BallTree
truth: Stop using KulsinskiDistance with BallTree; it is not a metric and is no longer supported.
quote: Remove support for `KulsinskiDistance` in :class:`neighbors.BallTree`. This dissimilarity is not a metric and cannot be supported by the BallTree.

subject: neighbors.NearestNeighbors
old: support for metrics other than euclidean and manhattan, and callables
truth: Stop using non-euclidean/non-manhattan metrics or callables in NearestNeighbors; they are deprecated and will be removed in 1.5.
quote: The support for metrics other than `euclidean` and `manhattan` and for callables in :class:`neighbors.NearestNeighbors` is deprecated and will be removed in version 1.5.

from_fact: support for metrics other than euclidean and manhattan, and callables

subject: multioutput.MultiOutputRegressor.partial_fit and MultiOutputClassifier.partial_fit
old: getattr before fit did not raise AttributeError
new: getattr before fit now raises AttributeError
truth: Calling getattr on partial_fit before calling fit now raises an AttributeError instead of silently returning something.
quote: :func:`getattr` on :meth:`multioutput.MultiOutputRegressor.partial_fit` and :meth:`multioutput.MultiOutputClassifier.partial_fit` now correctly raise an `AttributeError` if done before calling `fit`.

from_fact: getattr before fit did not raise attributeerror
getattr before fit now raises attributeerror

subject: pipeline.Pipeline.fit_transform
old: did not raise error when last step lacks fit_transform
new: now raises AttributeError if last step does not support fit_transform
truth: Pipeline.fit_transform now raises an AttributeError if the last step does not support fit_transform, instead of silently failing or behaving unexpectedly.
quote: :meth:`pipeline.Pipeline.fit_transform` now raises an `AttributeError` if the last step of the pipeline does not support `fit_transform`.

from_fact: did not raise error when last step lacks fit_transform
now raises attributeerror if last step does not support fit_transform

subject: preprocessing.KBinsDiscretizer
new: subsample parameter default will change to 200_000 in version 1.5 when strategy='kmeans' or strategy='uniform'
truth: Stop relying on subsample=None being the default for KBinsDiscretizer with strategy='kmeans' or 'uniform'; in version 1.5 the default becomes 200_000, which will silently subsample your data.
quote: The default value of the `subsample` parameter of :class:`preprocessing.KBinsDiscretizer` will change from `None` to `200_000` in version 1.5 when `strategy="kmeans"` or `strategy="uniform"`.

from_fact: subsample parameter default will change to 200_000 in version 1.5 when strategy='kmeans' or strategy='uniform'

subject: utils.check_array
old: behavior with pandas extension arrays and object dtypes
new: returns ndarray with object dtype for pandas DataFrames with extension arrays and object dtypes
truth: utils.check_array now returns an ndarray with object dtype when given pandas DataFrames with extension arrays and object dtypes, which may change the dtype of the output array.
quote: utils.check_array now supports pandas DataFrames with extension arrays and object dtypes by returning an ndarray with object dtype.

from_fact: behavior with pandas extension arrays and object dtypes
returns ndarray with object dtype for pandas dataframes with extension arrays and object dtypes

subject: instantiating classes inheriting from deprecated base classes
old: no warning when overriding __init__ on a deprecated base class
new: FutureWarning raised when instantiating a class that inherits from a deprecated base class and overrides __init__
truth: A FutureWarning is now raised when instantiating a class which inherits from a deprecated base class (decorated by utils.deprecated) and which overrides the __init__ method.
quote: |API| A `FutureWarning` is now raised when instantiating a class which inherits from

from_fact: no warning when overriding __init__ on a deprecated base class
futurewarning raised when instantiating a class that inherits from a deprecated base class and overrides __init__

subject: preprocessing.PolynomialFeatures
old: degree=0 with include_bias=False produced some output
new: degree=0 with include_bias=False raises an error; degree=0 with include_bias=True outputs a single constant array
truth: PolynomialFeatures with degree=0 now raises an error when include_bias=False, and outputs a single constant array when include_bias=True.
quote: will raise error when ``include_bias`` is set to False, and outputs a single

from_fact: degree=0 with include_bias=false produced some output
degree=0 with include_bias=false raises an error; degree=0 with include_bias=true outputs a single constant array

subject: cluster.KMeans default algorithm
new: "lloyd"
truth: Stop using algorithm="auto" or "full" in KMeans; they are deprecated aliases for "lloyd" and will be removed in version 1.3.
quote: ``"lloyd"`` which is the full classical EM-style algorithm.

subject: linear_model.Ridge, RidgeClassifier, ridge_regression tol default
new: tol=1e-4
truth: The default tolerance for Ridge, RidgeClassifier, and ridge_regression changed from 1e-3 to 1e-4, causing stricter convergence.
quote: The default value of `tol` was changed from `1e-3` to `1e-4` for :func:`linear_model.ridge_regression`, :class:`linear_model.Ridge` and :class:`linear_model.RidgeClassifier`.
